questions.question: Which component acts as the brain of a computer, executing instructions?

options.comment: The CPU fetches, decodes, and executes instructions — it is the core of all processing.

questions.question: What is the difference between hardware and software?

options.option: Hardware is physical; software is the programs that run on it
options.comment: Hardware = tangible components (monitor, keyboard). Software = instructions (OS, apps).

options.option: Hardware is temporary; software is permanently stored
Hardware handles output; software handles input only
Hardware is newer technology; software is older

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of system software?

options.comment: System software manages hardware and provides a platform for application software to run.

questions.question: A laptop is an example of which type of computer?

options.comment: Laptops are portable personal computers designed for individual use.

questions.question: What does the term 'input' mean in computing?

options.option: Data or instructions given to a computer by a user or device
options.comment: Input devices (keyboard, mouse, microphone) send data into the computer for processing.

options.option: The result produced by a computer after processing
The storage of data on a hard drive
The speed at which a processor runs

questions.question: Which is NOT a type of computer?

options.option: Router (it is a network device, not a computer type)
options.comment: Routers are networking devices. Computer types include desktops, laptops, tablets, and servers.

questions.question: What does RAM stand for?

options.comment: RAM is temporary, fast memory used to store data the CPU is actively using.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of RAM in a computer?

options.option: To temporarily store data the CPU is currently working with
options.comment: RAM is fast but volatile — data is lost when the computer is turned off.

options.option: To permanently store the operating system and files
To display graphics on the monitor
To connect the computer to the internet

questions.question: Which component is responsible for rendering images on screen?

options.comment: The GPU processes and renders visual data to display on the monitor.

questions.question: A keyboard is an example of which type of device?

options.comment: Input devices send data into the computer. Output devices (monitors, printers) receive data from it.

questions.question: What does the motherboard do?

options.option: Connects and allows communication between all hardware components
options.comment: The motherboard is the main circuit board — CPU, RAM, and all peripherals connect to it.

options.option: Provides power to external USB devices only
Controls the speed of the internet connection

questions.question: Which storage type is faster and has no moving parts?

options.comment: SSDs use flash memory chips. HDDs use spinning magnetic disks, which are slower.

questions.question: Which keyboard shortcut copies selected text?

options.comment: Ctrl+C copies the selection to the clipboard without removing it.

questions.question: What does the keyboard shortcut Ctrl + Z do?

options.comment: Ctrl+Z is the universal undo shortcut in most Windows applications.

questions.question: What does pressing Alt + Tab do in Windows?

options.comment: Alt+Tab shows a thumbnail of open windows so you can quickly switch between them.

options.option: Creates a new tab in the browser
Logs out of the current user account

questions.question: What does a double-click typically do?

options.option: Opens a file, folder, or application
options.comment: Double-clicking opens items. A single click selects; a right-click shows a context menu.

questions.question: Which key deletes the character to the LEFT of the cursor?

options.comment: Backspace deletes backwards (left). The Delete key deletes the character to the right.

questions.question: What does Ctrl + S do in most applications?

options.option: Saves the current file or document
options.comment: Ctrl+S is the universal save shortcut — use it frequently to avoid losing work.

options.option: Performs a search within the document

questions.question: What is the primary role of an operating system?

options.option: To manage hardware resources and provide a platform for applications
options.comment: The OS acts as intermediary between hardware and user-facing applications.

options.option: To browse the internet and display web pages
To store user documents and photos
To protect the computer from viruses only

questions.question: Which part of the Windows interface shows the Start button and running apps?

options.comment: The Taskbar runs along the bottom of the screen and shows pinned apps, open windows, and system icons.

questions.question: What is the Windows Desktop?

options.option: The main working area that appears after logging in
options.comment: The desktop is the background area where shortcuts, files, and the recycle bin are displayed.

options.option: A physical desk surface with a built-in screen
The folder where all user documents are stored

questions.question: Which Windows feature lets you quickly find apps and files by typing their name?

options.option: Windows Search (the search bar on the taskbar)
options.comment: Click the search icon or press the Windows key and type to find apps, files, or settings.

questions.question: To resize a window to fill the entire screen, you should:

options.option: Click the Maximise button (square icon in top-right corner)
options.comment: The three window control buttons are: Minimise (-), Maximise (square), and Close (X).

options.option: Right-click the taskbar and select Expand

questions.question: Which Windows utility shows all running processes and lets you end a frozen app?

options.option: Task Manager (opened with Ctrl + Shift + Esc)
options.comment: Task Manager shows CPU, memory, and disk usage per process. Use End Task to close a frozen app.

questions.question: What is the Windows application used to browse files and folders?

options.comment: File Explorer (formerly Windows Explorer) is the built-in file manager. Open it with Win + E.

questions.question: When you delete a file in Windows, where does it go first?

options.comment: Files go to the Recycle Bin first, allowing recovery. To permanently delete, empty the Recycle Bin.

options.option: A temporary hidden folder in C:\Windows

questions.question: What is the keyboard shortcut to paste copied or cut items?

options.comment: Ctrl+V pastes from the clipboard. Ctrl+C copies; Ctrl+X cuts (moves to clipboard).

questions.question: Which file extension indicates a Microsoft Word document?

options.comment: .docx is the default Word format. .xlsx = Excel, .pptx = PowerPoint, .pdf = PDF.

questions.question: To rename a file in Windows, you should:

options.option: Click once to select it, then press F2
options.comment: F2 activates rename mode. You can also right-click and select Rename.

options.option: Double-click the file and type the new name
Drag the file to the Desktop to rename it
Open the file and use Save As with the new name

questions.question: What does Ctrl + X do to a selected file?

options.option: Cuts the file (ready to be moved via Ctrl+V)
options.comment: Cut moves the file when pasted. Copy (Ctrl+C) leaves the original in place.

options.option: Permanently deletes the file without the Recycle Bin
Compresses the file into a ZIP archive
Closes the current File Explorer window

questions.question: What does 1 GB (Gigabyte) equal?

options.comment: Size order: KB (Kilobyte) < MB (Megabyte) < GB (Gigabyte) < TB (Terabyte).

questions.question: Which storage type holds data even when the computer is turned off?

options.comment: HDDs and SSDs are non-volatile — data persists without power. RAM is volatile and loses data.

questions.question: Before unplugging a USB flash drive, you should:

options.option: Use 'Safely Remove Hardware' to eject it first
options.comment: Ejecting ensures all write operations are complete and prevents data corruption.

options.option: Restart the computer and then remove it
Just pull it out — modern USB is always safe to remove
Delete all files on it before removing

questions.question: Which of these is an example of cloud storage?

options.comment: Cloud storage keeps files on remote servers accessible via the internet. Examples: Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox.

questions.question: A typical music file (MP3) is around 4 MB. How many songs fit on a 1 GB USB drive?
What is the advantage of an SSD over an HDD?

options.option: Faster read/write speeds and no moving parts
options.comment: SSDs are significantly faster and more shock-resistant. HDDs are cheaper per GB but slower.

options.option: SSDs store more data for less cost
SSDs can store data without electricity permanently
SSDs produce more heat than HDDs

questions.question: Which Windows menu is used to install and uninstall programs?

options.comment: In modern Windows, go to Settings > Apps > Installed Apps to manage your software.

questions.question: What does it mean to 'minimise' a window?

options.option: Hide it from view but keep it running on the taskbar
options.comment: Minimising keeps the app active but removes it from the visible screen area.

options.option: Close the application and stop it running
Make the window as small as possible on screen
Save the current work and pause the application

questions.question: Which keyboard shortcut opens a new window in File Explorer or a browser?

options.comment: Ctrl+N opens a New window (or new document in many apps). Ctrl+T opens a new tab in browsers.

questions.question: What is the function of the Windows Start Menu?

options.option: Access all installed apps, settings, search, and power options
options.comment: The Start Menu (Windows key) is the central launch point for all programs and system settings.

options.option: Show all currently open files and documents

questions.question: When an application is 'not responding', the best first step is:

options.option: Open Task Manager and use 'End Task'
options.comment: Task Manager (Ctrl+Shift+Esc) lets you force-close frozen apps without restarting the computer.

options.option: Pull out the power cord immediately
Click the application window repeatedly until it responds

questions.question: What does 'pinning' an app to the taskbar do?

options.option: Creates a permanent shortcut on the taskbar for quick access
options.comment: Pinned apps stay on the taskbar even when closed, giving you one-click access.

options.option: Prevents the app from being uninstalled
Runs the app at startup automatically
Locks the app so only the administrator can open it

questions.question: To print a document, which keyboard shortcut is used in most applications?

options.comment: Ctrl+P opens the Print dialog in virtually all Windows applications.

questions.question: Where can you check and manage documents waiting to be printed?

options.comment: Double-click the printer icon in the taskbar to view and manage the print queue.

questions.question: What should you do before physically connecting a new USB peripheral?

options.option: Nothing — modern USB is plug-and-play; just connect it
options.comment: USB is designed to be plug-and-play. Windows automatically detects and installs most devices.

options.option: Restart the computer in Safe Mode first
Uninstall all other USB drivers before connecting
Format the USB device before first use

questions.question: A scanner is used primarily to:

options.option: Convert physical paper documents into digital image files
options.comment: Scanners digitise paper documents so they can be saved, emailed, or edited on a computer.

options.option: Store files on a removable device
Test the speed of the internet connection

questions.question: Which port is most commonly used to connect modern peripheral devices?

options.comment: USB is the standard for keyboards, mice, printers, flash drives, and most peripherals.

questions.question: If a printer shows 'Offline' status, the first step to fix it is:

options.option: Check that the printer is powered on and connected
options.comment: 'Offline' usually means the printer is off, out of paper, or disconnected from the network/USB.

options.option: Reinstall Windows to reset printer drivers
Delete all pending print jobs and restart the router
Replace the printer ink before doing anything else

questions.question: What is the most effective first step when a computer behaves unexpectedly?

options.comment: Restarting clears RAM, stops stuck processes, and applies pending updates — it fixes most common issues.

options.option: Open Device Manager and update all drivers

questions.question: How do you open Task Manager directly without the right-click menu?

options.comment: Ctrl+Shift+Esc opens Task Manager directly. Ctrl+Alt+Del also provides access but shows a menu first.

questions.question: Why is it important to install Windows updates regularly?

options.option: Updates patch security vulnerabilities and fix software bugs
options.comment: Unpatched systems are vulnerable to malware and exploits. Updates also improve performance.

options.option: Updates increase the computer's RAM automatically
Updates are only for adding new features, not security
Updates slow down older computers and should be avoided

questions.question: A program shows an error message and closes. The BEST first response is:

options.option: Note the error message, restart the app, and try again
options.comment: Error messages contain useful diagnostic information. Try reproducing the issue after restarting.

options.option: Format the hard drive and reinstall Windows
Turn off the computer immediately by holding the power button

questions.question: Your computer is running slowly. Which task is most likely to help?

options.option: Close unnecessary background programs via Task Manager
options.comment: Too many running apps consume RAM and CPU. Closing unneeded ones frees up resources.

questions.question: What does an 'error code' in a Windows error message help you do?

options.option: Search for the specific problem and solution online
options.comment: Error codes like 0x80070005 are unique identifiers. Searching them reveals the exact cause.

options.option: Identify which hardware needs to be replaced
Call the manufacturer for a free hardware replacement
Determine when the computer was last turned on

questions.question: What does WiFi stand for?

options.comment: WiFi is the trademark name for IEEE 802.11 wireless networking standards.

questions.question: What device connects your home devices to the internet?

options.comment: A router directs traffic between your local network (LAN) and the internet (WAN).

questions.question: What is a WiFi password used for?

options.option: To prevent unauthorised devices from joining the network
options.comment: WiFi security (WPA2/WPA3) encrypts traffic and restricts access to authorised users.

options.option: To protect files on the hard drive from being read
To limit how many websites you can visit per day
To speed up the internet connection

questions.question: What does an IP address identify?

options.option: A device's unique address on a network
options.comment: Every device on a network has an IP address so data can be routed to the right destination.

options.option: The speed of the internet connection
The brand and model of the router
The number of devices connected to WiFi

questions.question: If your WiFi shows 'Connected' but no internet access, the best first step is:

options.option: Restart the router by unplugging it for 10 seconds
options.comment: Most home internet issues are resolved by restarting the router, which refreshes the connection.

questions.question: What is the difference between a LAN and the internet?

options.option: LAN is a local private network; the internet is a global public network
options.comment: LAN (Local Area Network) connects devices in one location. The internet is the worldwide network.

options.option: LAN is wireless; the internet requires a cable
LAN is faster than the internet in all cases
The internet and LAN are the same thing

questions.question: Which of the following is a strong password?

options.comment: Strong passwords mix uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols, and are at least 12 characters long.

questions.question: What is the purpose of antivirus software?

options.option: To detect, block, and remove malicious software (malware)
options.comment: Antivirus scans for known threats, monitors suspicious behaviour, and removes infections.

options.option: To speed up the computer by removing old files
To back up your files to an external drive

questions.question: What is malware?

options.option: Software designed to harm, steal data, or gain unauthorised access
options.comment: Malware includes viruses, ransomware, spyware, and trojans — all designed with malicious intent.

options.option: Software that is poorly designed but not harmful
A type of hardware failure caused by overheating
An email attachment sent accidentally to the wrong person

questions.question: Which habit helps most to keep your computer secure?

options.option: Keeping your OS and applications updated
options.comment: Software updates patch security holes. Outdated software is the most common entry point for attacks.

options.option: Using the same password for all accounts for easy memory
Downloading software from any website you find online
Sharing your login details with trusted colleagues

questions.question: What should you do if you receive a suspicious email asking for your password?

options.option: Delete it — legitimate organisations never ask for passwords via email
options.comment: This is a phishing attack. Banks and companies will never request your password by email.

options.option: Reply with your password but change it afterwards
Forward it to all your contacts as a warning
Click the link and enter fake details to test it

questions.question: Why should you lock your computer screen when stepping away?

options.option: To prevent others from accessing your files and applications
options.comment: Press Win+L to lock instantly. Prevents unauthorised access in shared office environments.

options.option: To save electricity by turning off the monitor
To stop Windows updates from installing while you are away
Locking has no security benefit — it only saves the screensaver

questions.question: Where in Windows do you go to change the screen resolution?

options.comment: Display Settings lets you change resolution, brightness, orientation, and scale.

questions.question: What is a 'startup program' in Windows?

options.option: An application that launches automatically when Windows starts
options.comment: Too many startup programs slow boot time. Manage them in Task Manager > Startup Apps.

options.option: The first program ever installed on the computer
A program that runs only when the computer is first set up

questions.question: Windows accessibility features can help users who:

options.option: Have visual, hearing, or motor difficulties
options.comment: Accessibility options include Magnifier, Narrator (screen reader), high contrast, and sticky keys.

options.option: Want to make the computer run faster
Want to connect to more WiFi networks simultaneously
Want to install more applications without a password

questions.question: How do you create a shortcut on the Windows Desktop for an application?

options.option: Right-click the app in the Start Menu and select 'Send to > Desktop'
options.comment: You can also drag an app from the Start Menu to the desktop or right-click and create shortcut.

options.option: Copy the program's .exe file directly onto the desktop
Click and drag the application's title bar to the desktop
Open the app and press Ctrl+D to pin it to the desktop

questions.question: What does increasing the 'text size' in Display Settings do?

options.option: Makes text larger across Windows and apps without changing resolution
options.comment: Scaling text size improves readability, especially on high-resolution (4K) displays.

options.option: Changes the font used in all documents to a larger typeface
Increases the screen resolution to fit more content
Changes the brightness of the screen backlight

questions.question: To stop an unwanted program from opening every time Windows starts, go to:

options.option: Task Manager > Startup Apps, then Disable it
options.comment: Task Manager's Startup tab shows all auto-starting apps. Disabling speeds up boot time.

options.option: File Explorer > C:\Windows\System32 and delete the file
